dungeon: (HUGE MULTI-DUNGEON STREAM ~100 entries) ROTF — Stonetaker (new boss) + Dwarf King taunts (likely a naming correction)
source: video disc_PRzNeGfRR00 (1072 frames, 300 transitions = ~100 entries, 1609 chat; client X1.1). Very diverse dungeons; chat captured two bosses' taunt sets.
map: DOCUMENTED biomes: blue WATER arenas, tan DESERT, green grass realm, grey CASTLE STONE, brown dirt paths, white SNOW, and RED/MAGENTA arenas (with blue snowflake projectiles — Stonetaker's domain).

bosses.name: Stonetaker
bosses.note: DOCUMENTED VERBATIM (boss-colored chat). A NEW stone-HOARDER boss (golem/troll?) obsessed with its stones/gems — admires them ('Beautiful stones'), warns players off ('Leave my stones in peace'), then panics on death ('No... no... NO!', 'LEAVE MY STONES ALONE!'). Fought in a red/magenta arena.

bosses.name: Dwarf King
bosses.note: DOCUMENTED VERBATIM. IMPORTANT: this taunt ('You'll taste my axe!') is the one currently attached to the BUILT 'Death King' boss (OCBosses 0x8fd3) — so that boss is most likely actually named 'DWARF KING' (an axe-wielding dwarf, not a 'Death King'). PROBABLE NAMING CORRECTION for the build.

mechanics: DOCUMENTED: (1) Stonetaker = a new stone-hoarder boss with a full taunt arc (admire -> warn -> death-panic). (2) NAMING CORRECTION lead: 'Dwarf King' speaks 'You'll taste my axe!' — the line I attached to 'Death King' (0x8fd3); the boss is likely 'Dwarf King'. (3) Coin-analyze -> Fame reconfirmed ('You have analyzed the coin, discovering [Fame]'). (4) 'You have been granted an additional [...]' reward line. Client X1.1.
uncertain: Stonetaker's attacks/HP/dungeon not isolated (only taunts); some lines truncated at crop edge. Dwarf-King-vs-Death-King: high-confidence it's 'Dwarf King' (axe + the matching taunt) but worth a second confirmation before renaming the built boss. ~100 entries not individually named.
confidence: HIGH for the Stonetaker taunt set (new boss) + the Dwarf King taunt (verbatim). MEDIUM-HIGH that 'Death King' (built) should be renamed 'Dwarf King'. LOW for dungeon mapping/drops.
